nodes.label: AI as literature review accelerator
nodes.grounding_span: AI is invaluable for me in the planning stage of my projects

nodes.label: AI as coding assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I would definitely not use online tutorials to code and I would use exclusively AI tools

nodes.label: AI reliability in citations
nodes.grounding_span: some current tools can make errors when providing citations

nodes.label: AI in data interpretation and hypothesis building
nodes.grounding_span: I am still dubious of existing tools' capabilities for data interpretation and hypothesis building

nodes.label: AI as experiment planning collaborator
nodes.grounding_span: planning experiments, I think this is good when it is done in collaboration with an AI tool, but never solely by an AI tool

nodes.label: Research efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: would have saved me weeks of work

nodes.label: Epistemic trust and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I always need to double check

nodes.label: Intellectual ownership and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: you want to know exactly what is going on in your project which you lose track of if you don't plan it yourself

nodes.label: Creative and analytical engagement
nodes.grounding_span: Hypothesis building again is firstly a fun process that I enjoy

nodes.label: Positive toward AI for literature review and coding
nodes.grounding_span: AI is invaluable for me in the planning stage of my projects

nodes.label: Dubious toward AI for data interpretation and hypothesis building
nodes.grounding_span: I am still dubious of existing tools' capabilities for data interpretation and hypothesis building

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI for experiment planning
nodes.grounding_span: planning experiments, I think this is good when it is done in collaboration with an AI tool, but never solely by an AI tool

nodes.label: Desire for reliable citations
nodes.grounding_span: If I could rely 100% on citations this would be a game changer
